title: OpenSIN Team Manifest
description: Metadata manifest for a Team-SIN-* marketplace bundle. One file per Team-SIN-* repo. Aggregated into oh-my-sin.json for the marketplace frontend. Decided in Wave 4 — Option A.

$schema_version.description: Manifest format version. Bump requires updating this schema.

id.description: Canonical repo name. Must match the GitHub repo under OpenSIN-AI/.

name.description: Display name in the marketplace card.

tier.description: marketplace = paid add-on Mini-Abo. core-included = shipped free in every Pro plan. enterprise = contact-sales only.

status.description: Lifecycle stage. Drives marketplace UI badges and whether the team is listable.

tagline.description: One-line pitch shown on the marketplace card.

summary.description: Paragraph-length description for the product page.

bullets.description: Feature bullets for the product page.

screenshot.description: Relative path inside the Team-SIN-* repo, e.g. assets/screenshot.png. Optional.

agents.description: A2A-SIN-* agent repos that belong to this team. Source of truth for which capabilities the buyer unlocks. Empty only when status=coming-soon.

id.description: Canonical A2A-SIN-* repo name. Must exist and not be archived.

role.description: primary = flagship capability. supporting = required co-agent. optional = extra capability the user can toggle.

summary.description: One-line role inside this team (not the agent's general purpose).

model.description: free-with-pro = unlocked for any Pro tier. monthly-addon = flat Mini-Abo. metered = usage-priced. enterprise-quote = contact sales.

stripe_product_id.description: Stripe product id created for this team. Optional during beta.

permissions.description: Capabilities the team's agents require. Shown on the purchase page for transparency.

budgets.description: Recommended default spend limits; user can override in chat.opensin.ai.

decided_in.description: PR or doc anchor that locked this team's definition.

owner_team.description: GitHub team handle owning the manifest.
